era: 1987-present. Metal/Grunge. E=9 -- grunge-metal synthesis, emotional directness, earned darkness. Beacon Hill, Seattle. Columbia Records.
active: 1987-present (hiatus 2002-2005 following Staley's death; William DuVall joined 2006)
note_staley: Layne Staley (August 22, 1967 - April 5, 2002). Died of speedball overdose (heroin and cocaine) in Seattle, age 34. Body discovered two weeks after death. April 5 is also Kurt Cobain's death date (1994), eight years earlier. Remembered here with full respect.

theory_nodes.concept: Cantrell-Staley guitar-vocal harmony: the most immediately identifiable feature of the AIC sound. Guitar riff written for harmony with vocal melody. Intervals including thirds, fifths, minor seconds, tritones -- mildly dissonant, creating unsettled quality. Unique compositional approach: metal band with vocal harmony group structure.
theory_nodes.description: Guitar-vocal harmony system: the specific AIC differentiator.

theory_nodes.concept: Dirt (1992): direct autobiography of addiction. Not metaphorical. Rooster = Cantrell's father Vietnam War. Would = Andrew Wood tribute. Down in a Hole = Staley's deepest statement. 53 minutes of sustained emotional weight. Four-times platinum. The most emotionally intense record of the grunge era.
theory_nodes.description: The grunge era's most autobiographically intense record.

theory_nodes.concept: Metal Summit closure. E=9 mechanism: grunge-metal synthesis (emotional directness + metal weight) vs. sludge weight (Melvins) vs. sustained vocal-guitar intensity (Soundgarden). AIC's E=9 is qualitative: earned darkness, real biography, melodic heaviness. The Summit's final and most emotionally complete statement.
theory_nodes.description: Metal Summit position 4 of 4. The Summit closes.

cross_references.relationship: Metal Summit positions 3 and 4. Both E=9. Both Seattle. Contemporaneous major-label 1992-1994. Cornell and Staley -- two of Seattle's most important vocalists, both died young, both treated respectfully.

Metal Summit positions 2 and 4. Both E=9. AIC formed in 1987, four years after Melvins. AIC absorbed the sludge-metal down-tuned vocabulary (from Melvins and wider tradition) and combined it with melody.

E=9 + E=9 = E=18. The most dramatically interrupted natural event (wolf howl cut by lightning) pairs with the most melodically dark human music (AIC's guitar-vocal harmony system in earned darkness). Both defined by the combination of held melodic beauty and sudden violent interruption.

milestones: DIRT -- MOST EMOTIONALLY INTENSE GRUNGE RECORD
